snippets.title: Confusion matrix with scikit-learn
snippets.code:
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.datasets import make_classification
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

X, y = make_classification(n_samples=500, weights=[0.7, 0.3], random_state=0)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=0)

clf = LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000)
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = (clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1] >= 0.5).astype(int)

cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
print("Rows=actual, cols=predicted:")
print(cm)
# [[TN, FP],
#  [FN, TP]]  for labels [0, 1]

tn, fp, fn, tp = cm.ravel()
print(f"TP={tp}, FP={fp}, TN={tn}, FN={fn}")
snippets.explanation: Standard workflow: threshold probabilistic scores, then confusion_matrix on held-out labels. ravel() unpacks TN, FP, FN, TP in sklearn order for binary labels [0, 1].

snippets.title: Manual TP/FP/TN/FN counts
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

y_true = np.array([1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0])
y_pred = np.array([1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0])

# Positive class = 1
tp = np.sum((y_true == 1) & (y_pred == 1))
fp = np.sum((y_true == 0) & (y_pred == 1))
tn = np.sum((y_true == 0) & (y_pred == 0))
fn = np.sum((y_true == 1) & (y_pred == 0))

assert tp + fp + tn + fn == len(y_true)
print(f"TP={tp}, FP={fp}, TN={tn}, FN={fn}")
snippets.explanation: Boolean masks make the four definitions explicit and verify they sum to N — useful in interviews and custom metrics pipelines.

snippets.title: Rates from one matrix
snippets.code:
tp, fp, tn, fn = 42, 8, 850, 10
n = tp + fp + tn + fn

accuracy = (tp + tn) / n
tpr = tp / (tp + fn)       # recall / sensitivity
fpr = fp / (fp + tn)
specificity = tn / (tn + fp)
precision = tp / (tp + fp)

print(f"Accuracy: {accuracy:.3f}")
print(f"TPR (recall): {tpr:.3f}")
print(f"FPR: {fpr:.3f}")
print(f"Precision: {precision:.3f}")
snippets.explanation: All common binary rates are algebraic functions of the four counts. Precision/recall/F1 depth lives in the Precision/Recall/F1 topic.

snippets.title: Matrix at a custom threshold
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

y_true = np.array([1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 0])
scores = np.array([0.9, 0.2, 0.55, 0.3, 0.6, 0.4, 0.45, 0.1])

for t in [0.3, 0.5, 0.7]:
    y_pred = (scores >= t).astype(int)
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
    print(f"threshold={t}: TP={cm[1,1]}, FP={cm[0,1]}, TN={cm[0,0]}, FN={cm[1,0]}")
snippets.explanation: Each threshold induces a different matrix — the same scores can yield very different TP/FP/TN/FN when the cutoff moves.
